matches.rationale: Same Edit action and README-path prerequisite; the extracted path regex is narrower than the gold's arbitrary subdirectory scope.
Same Write action and README-path prerequisite; the extracted path regex is narrower than the gold's arbitrary subdirectory scope.
Targets edits to Next.js source/tests and requires starting the same watch command first, including the already-running/justify-skip qualification.
Targets the full build requirement before CI push. It omits the branch/bootstrap and multi-package clauses of the compound gold rule.
Directly targets git branch switching and requires the full bootstrap build afterward.
Same Write target: creation of test-suite files must go through pnpm new-test rather than manual creation.
Targets rerunning an unchanged test command and requires analysis of saved output; it is broader than the gold's multiple-grep scenario but covers the same action.
Same Edit target: setTimeout-based waiting in tests is replaced by next-test-utils retry().
Same Edit target: deprecated check() calls are prohibited in favor of retry() with expect().
Same Edit target: an inline files object in test setup should be replaced by a real fixture directory.

Targets local CI reproduction test commands and requires matching CI mode/environment variables. The evidence also mentions module-resolution fixes, but CI environment reproduction is the closest single gold rule.
Same Bash action for creating/editing a PR description and the same required trailing NEXT_JS_LLM_PR marker.
Same Bash commit target and prohibition on committing local secret files; the placeholder-only documentation clause is omitted.
Same Bash target: changing a Git remote to HTTPS after SSH failure is prohibited absent explicit user request.
Same Bash target and lightweight SSH preflight before force-push/rebase operations that may hydrate partial-clone objects.
Same Bash commit-content target and prohibition on Generated with Claude Code or Claude co-author footers.
Same Bash commit trigger and requirement to pre-run the specified formatter/linter checks on changed files.
Same Bash target: gh pr ready is prohibited so the PR remains a draft for the user.
Same integration-test Bash commands and prerequisite rebuild when Next.js source has changed.
Same Edit target for a new feature flag and its type/schema/define-env wiring; the extraction covers the gold's compound wiring requirement.
Same Edit target: define-env must force Node-import-gating flags false for edge builds.
Same Edit target: react-server-dom-webpack imports stay in entry-base and are consumed via exported modules elsewhere.
Same Edit target: relative internal require() in app-page.ts is prohibited and access is routed through entry-base exports.
Same Edit target: browser.back() must not be used to return to the router-act page with visible accordion links.
Same Edit target: avoid new Rust super:: imports, with the same inline-mod exception.
Same Edit target: highlight attributes in documentation code blocks require counting actual lines; the extraction omits explicitly selecting only relevant lines.

misses.note: The compiler emitted the watch prerequisite only for Edit; it emitted no corresponding Write rule for creating source or integration-test files.
The instruction to keep a background watch process running while iterating was not extracted as a Bash lifecycle rule.
Compiler skip: "Mode-specific test command list (dev/prod × turbo/webpack) is a usage catalog, not a rule".
The prose instruction to terminate a watch process at session completion was not bound to a Bash trigger.
Compiler skip: "Batching related edits before one rebuild, and using `pnpm --filter=next types` for faster type-only checks, are workflow efficiency tips without obligation/prohibition wording".
Compiler skip: "Choosing `pnpm --filter=next build` vs `pnpm build-all` based on whether a change is core-only or spans multiple packages requires contextual judgment, not a syntactic trigger".
Compiler skip: "Bundler selection section (Turbopack default, --webpack flag, no --no-turbopack flag) is factual documentation, not a rule".
Compiler skip: "Bundler selection section (Turbopack default, --webpack flag, no --no-turbopack flag) is factual documentation, not a rule".
The compiler represented mandatory pnpm new-test generation only as a Write rule, not as the gold's Bash command rule.
No Bash rule was extracted for AI-agent noninteractive pnpm new-test invocation and its --args argument format.
No Bash rule was extracted requiring scripts/pr-status.js in response to PR-status, review, or CI-failure requests.
Compiler skip: "PR-triage prioritization mindset (build/lint/types before tests; treat failures as real until disproven) is general judgment, not tied to a specific tool call".
Compiler skip: "PR-triage prioritization mindset (build/lint/types before tests; treat failures as real until disproven) is general judgment, not tied to a specific tool call".

Compiler skip: "Verifying edge-bundle regressions with a specific standalone test, and using mode-specific test commands 'when the fix concerns module resolution,' both require judging what kind of change was made, not syntactically triggerable".
The required automated-triage behavior is delegated to `.agents/skills/pr-status-triage/SKILL.md`; no Bash rule for invoking that skill was extracted.

The extracted security-review rule uses Bash (gh pr review commands), while the gold rule requires Edit; strict same-tool matching therefore fails.

unmatched_extracted.note: The document contains a real Bash rule not to refetch the same gh api/curl data for alternate analyses, but gold has no corresponding rule.
This is a real Bash-bindable PR-review rule in the document. Gold lists only an Edit-targeted version, which cannot match under the same-tool criterion.

The document contains a distinct real Edit rule to wire runtime environment values in next-server.ts/export worker when a flag is used inside compiled runtime internals; gold does not list that target.
The document contains a real Edit rule requiring require() calls to remain inside compile-time if/else branches for DCE; gold does not list it.
